must_detect.matched_text:
Internal Compliance Memo — South Coast Renewables Ltd (ACN 000 000 000; ABN 00 000 000 000).
Purpose: update on policy refresh covering crypto, ESG/climate, cyber, insider-list, information-barrier, blackout, tipping, and commercial terms in an Australian context.
Regulatory framing: references align to Corporations law concepts and the Sydney Listing Exchange (SLE) guidance under continuous disclosure principles, with oversight by the Corporate Markets Authority (CMA) (both fictional).
Crypto education: Marketing will host “Crypto for Energy Billing” (token code CRP-TEST-01) as a product explainer only; no token sale, no financial product promotion, and no new market-sensitive features.
ESG/climate materials: The brochure recycles metrics from our public FY24 Sustainability Statement dated 30 June FY24; no new targets or unannounced climate commitments are included.
Cyber tabletop: IT will run a simulated “cyber incident” drill using placeholder fields only (ISIN XX0000000000; Account 000-000-000; BSB 000-000) and no live customer or employee systems.
Employment/health: HR is piloting a generic WHS self-declaration form template and fitness-for-duty checklist; this memo contains no individual health information; queries to hr-queries@screnewables.example.au.
Credit/operations: Retail credit assessment and direct debit mandate templates use dummy identifiers (Client ID CL-0000, ABN 00 000 000 000) and carry a watermark “Not an offer”; these are process examples only.
Insider-list training: Compliance will demonstrate list creation using fictitious “Project Eucalypt,” with no real insiders or securities mentioned and storage in the Compliance Vault sandbox.
Information barriers: Research, finance, and operations remain ring-fenced; cross-team queries must route via legal-ops@screnewables.example.au; do not forward internal packs outside your ring.
Blackout and anti-tipping: The close-period schedule is unchanged; staff must avoid commenting on results, crypto pilots, or commercial terms on social media per Policy CO-IB-03.
For precision: the term “commercial terms” in this memo refers to redacted templates and learning examples; there is no live pricing, counterparties, or negotiation content herein.
Public contact points: CMA Enquiries (fictional) 1800 000 000 and SLE InfoDesk (fictional) 1300 000 000 remain available for general guidance; internal questions to compliance@screnewables.example.au.
Board-only note: a draft term sheet for a climate-linked power aggregation with Mallee Grid Co. Pty Ltd is under review and not generally available; continuous disclosure assessment is in progress under SLE 3.1-style principles.
Handling for the above: add relevant staff to the insider list and maintain information barriers until diligence completes and a disclosure determination is made.
All other references to crypto, ESG/climate, cyber incidents, insider lists, information barriers, blackout, tipping, and commercial terms in this memo are training, marketing, or operations-only and non-market sensitive.

uncertain.reason: ISINs can be sensitive instrument identifiers but are not included in the allowed rule set here.
Internal token/test code may indicate a DPT/crypto asset but there is no specific allowed rule for such identifiers.
